title: What is Ritsuko holding
wikitext:
[]: What is Ritsuko holding?             Quote Post by 0x2eleven » Mon Jun 07, 2010 4:19 am Does anyone know what this thing is and what its significance is? We see it in episode 15 when Ritsuko, Misato, and Kaji are having drinks together. Kaji passes it to Ritsuko and remarks, "Here's your cat." It happens at 13:18 in my version.

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Mon Jun 07, 2010 5:48 am Looks like something to attach to the cat's collar.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 07, 2010 7:58 am There was this theory that it had to do with Kaji's visit with the cat lady earlier in that episode.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Jun 07, 2010 9:11 am He actually says, "It's a cat souvenir". He knows that she collects cat trinkets like the ones on her desk. It's her reaction that's important: RITSUKO: What were you doing in Kyoto? Kaji: Huh? That's from Matsushiro. RITSUKO: It's no use playing dumb. Your fingers will get burned if you get involved in this too deeply. That's the advice of a friend.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Mon Jun 07, 2010 9:23 am My theory is very much like thewayneiac.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 07, 2010 4:13 pm It is a very odd exchange... Even if something about the charm did tell Ritsuko where it came from, why would it also tell her what Kaji was investigating? I think it's more likely she (and Gendo) already knew exactly what Kaji has been up to and used this moment to deliver a warning in such a way that the commander could continue to pretend ignorance and wouldn't be forced to act. On the other hand, is this what ultimately leads to Kaji being removed from the combat roster sometime before ep19? I agree that the grandma's cat conversation probably wasn't a code, but it's still a very odd scene. Something symbolic and/or foreshadowy is going on there but It just doesn't quite click into place. Also, this feels like it should probably be moved to discussion

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Mon Jun 07, 2010 6:49 pm Cats are a symbol to neurotic and lonely women, that is exactly what Ritsuko is.
